strengths: Current hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work at re:cinq — building MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows, directly on-target for the role's agentic architecture mandate

Deep distributed-systems credentials: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O, caching, and real-time data at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, plus a self-built event-driven geo-data platform running on Kubernetes since 2016

Regulated-fintech exposure at RIDE Capital (German investment platform, with TDD/CI/CD/trunk-based discipline) and pricing-systems ownership at Optilyz, backed by ~18 years of full-stack engineering breadth and founder experience
concerns: No demonstrated executive or organizational leadership — career history is senior/staff IC roles with no evidence of scaling teams from 10 to 30+ or developing a technical-leadership layer
Agentic-AI experience is recent (April 2025 onward) and tooling-oriented; no documented production multi-agent systems operating with direct financial consequences
No evidence of governing outsourced/hybrid engineering models or communicating technical roadmaps at the board level

key_factors.factor: High-scale distributed-systems and real-time data expertise

response.justification: Bogdan Szabo combines the two competencies this role most depends on: current, hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work (MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq) and genuinely deep distributed-systems experience — low-latency microservices at millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic and an event-driven platform he founded and has operated on Kubernetes since 2016. His time at RIDE Capital, a regulated German fintech where he applied TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development, plus pricing-optimization work at Optilyz, maps well onto the compliance-aware, disciplined-shipping culture the position requires. Nearly two decades of engineering breadth, founder experience, and a sustained record of teaching and mentoring through workshops, dojos, and courses signal the technical authority and communication ability to grow into the executive dimensions of the role.
